## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Issue to Resolve

Feature Request: Implement 'User Action' Functionality Similar to AnkiMobile
In AnkiMobile (starting from version 2.0.88+), there is a feature that allows users to trigger JavaScript in the review screen. This advanced functionality is especially beneficial for those using shared decks that provide keyboard shortcuts to perform actions, such as revealing a hint or the next cloze deletion. By defining a user action, it's possible to trigger these actions with external devices, like a gamepad, or even by using gestures on the screen.

For instance, in AnkiMobile, if a card template provides a revealNext() function, users can instruct AnkiMobile to call it with the following code:

```
var userJs1 = revealNext;
```

This feature is especially handy for those who have written their own actions or are using certain shared decks/templates that provide these actions.

For more details, please refer to the [AnkiMobile documentation](https://docs.ankimobile.net/study-tools.html). Once on the page, scroll down to the "User Action 1-8" section for specifics.

## Issue to Resolve

Instant.parse(..) inconsistent between jvm and ios targets
From [ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET format](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime.format/-date-time-components/-formats/-i-s-o_-d-a-t-e_-t-i-m-e_-o-f-f-s-e-t.html):

> .., except that during parsing, specifying the minutes is optional.
>
> This format differs from [LocalTime.Formats.ISO](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-local-time/-formats/-i-s-o.html) in its time part in that specifying the seconds is not optional.

JVM allows to ommit seconds (i.e. parse "2024-03-19T17:50Z") while builds for ios do not (fails with `kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeFormatException: Parse error at char 16: expected char ':', got 'Z`)

Omitting minutes, while allowed as per documentation, always fails for both targets.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thanks! The issue is that, on the JVM and the JS, we delegate to the platform-specific implementation of `OffsetDateTime.parse`, which does allow skipping the seconds: https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/OffsetDateTime.html#parse-java.lang.CharSequence- We do that to work around the older JVM versions not supporting parsing arbitrary offsets, only `Z`.

The solution seems to be to stop relying on the platform implementation and use our version of `ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET` throughout.

Also, "omitting minutes" refers to the minutes of the UTC offset, that is, you can just have `+03` instead of `+03:00`.
regarding minutes: my bad, can't read 🤦 

It would be nice if both targets would behave equally, especially since folks tend to test on jvm/android and just expect (or hope) that ios will just work the same way. But, if read correctly, the documentation is right. Generally it is not allowed to ommit the seconds part.

API reference improvements
It would be nice to systematically and extensively improve our API reference:

For every API endpoint, it would be nice to provide the following (example is the place where the property does not hold):

* Extensively document what the entity represents, whether it follows ISO-8601, **how the entity can be acquired**, and what time scale it comes from. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-day-of-week/) 
* Provide a minimum of how the entity can be used. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-fixed-offset-time-zone/)
* Thoroughly explain the allowed and expected contract and behaviours and system-specifics. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-clock/)
* If applicable, provide contexts in which the entity is expected/recommended to be used (e.g. our `README` shines at that) and how it interacts with other (core) entities. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-local-date-time/)
* Provide a basic usage example. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-time-zone/)
* If applicable, provide details about conversion rules, gotchas and recommendations. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-instant/)
* Where appropriate, information about conversions and interactions with native types (e.g. Instant <-> Java Instant conversion)
* Ensure that information used in our `README` is, in fact, deducible from the core entities documentation

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

> If applicable, provide details about conversion rules, gotchas and recommendations. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-instant/)

I don't know of gotchas related to `Instant`. The only one that comes to mind is that `instant + 1.days` is a non-calendar operation (this could be worth documenting in the `plus` function), but I'd argue it's `Duration`'s gotcha. `Instant` seems tough to use incorrectly to me. What gotchas did you have in mind?

> Where appropriate, information about conversions and interactions with native types (e.g. Instant <-> Java Instant conversion)

When you have specific behavior on some platforms, you document it in the common code, makes sense; but when you have some extra interactions with platform code, you can't document them in common code (because symbols aren't resolved). If you have platform-specific documentation, in the IDE, it is either ignored (when writing common code) or replaces the common documentation. In the latter case, you have to repeat the common documentation for platforms *and* include platform-specific additions, which I think just isn't worth it.

If the system was a bit different and platform-specific documentation was *added* to the common documentation (and maybe the IDE would be smart enough to show the documentation for the platforms the project actually uses), then I agree that mentioning platform-specific converters and so on would be beneficial. Or better yet, if there was "extension documentation" along with extension functions: then we'd be able to show `NSDate` conversions in the `Instant` documentation for Darwin targets.

## Issue to Resolve

A number of bugs and inconsistencies with Java
https://gist.github.com/ilma4/a5ad5b5c284969e24f4e2149a2f8b273 lists several bugs and inconsistencies.

- [ ] `kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.parse(s) vs LocalDate.Formats.ISO.parse(s)` (and the same for `LocalDateTime`): Java's facilities don't support having too many leading zeroes, but our parsing mechanism does, and delegating to Java prevents us from reading strings like `+00000000000002022-07-16`.
- [ ] `DatePeriod.parse and DateTimePeriod.parse parsing "P"`: it is inconsistent with ISO 8601 to parse just `P` without any components, so we should probably fix that.
- [x] `kotlinx.datetime.DatePeriod is always normalized unlike `java.time.Duration`: this is intentional.
- [x] `kotlinx.datetime.Instant.parse`: intentional.
- [x] `Different periods between two dates in Java and Kotlin`: looks like Java's bug.
- [x] `kotlin.datetime.Duration max value is different from Java's`: intentional.
- [ ] `kotlin.time.Duration.parseIsoString with too many digits`: it is a bug that we think `PT0000000000000000000000000M` is an infinite duration. (Not this library's scope, though)
- [ ] `kotlin.time.Duration.parse("PT+-2H") successfully parses -2 hours`: not valid ISO 8601, we can fix that. (Not this library's scope, though)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/pull/5370 is the `kotlin.time.Duration` portion of the fixes.

### App version

6.804

### Problem you are trying to solve

I'd love some way to mark things as read when archiving.

Generally, when archiving something unread, it's because I have sufficient information from the subject line/already roughly know the contents - or know I don't care too much about it, but still want it "just in case". I like being able to filter by unread though, especially if it's something I want to get back to later and refile - but don't want in my inbox because it's not really a priority. 

### Suggested solution

An option to mark things read when archiving, like what's done with spam, would be great. Alternatively I think it'd be similarly nice to have one of the possible swipe actions be "mark as read and archive" together.

### Screenshots / Drawings / Technical details

_No response_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Honestly I think this should be the default. I can't think of a use case to archive a message and not also mark it read.

## Issue to Resolve

Unified inbox doesn't show up
- [ ] ### Checklist

- [x] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [x] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App

Thunderbird for Android

### App version

8.2

### Where did you get the app from?

Google Play

### Android version

15

### Device model

OnePlus 11 5G

### Steps to reproduce

1. I uninstalled the Thunderbird app on my phone since it was not getting new emails, while my Gmail and Outlook programs were getting it.
2. So, I re-installed Thunderbird from Google Play Store.
3. I opened Thunderbird after installation and added the first email account from Gmail. I did not see the Unified box, but I thought maybe it's because there's only one email account added. But the gmail account was receiving emails.
4. So I proceeded to add a second email account from Gmail and to my surprise, I still did not see a Unified box.
5. I added a third email account from Outlook and still no Unified box showed up, even though each individual account was getting and showing it's emails from the email server.
6. I then closed the Thunderbird app and re- opened it.
7. To my shock and surprise the Unified box was now showing after some frustrating steps.

In my view, the Unified box should always be there whether there's one email account or more than one. OR you could not show the Unified box if only one account is added, but automatically show it as soon as the second account is added. The first option of always showing Unified box seems more intuitive to me and a better option for your app users.

### Expected behavior

The Unified box should have been there as soon as an email account was added.

### Actual behavior

No Unified box showed up after adding the first email account and after adding the next two accounts.

### Logs

_No response_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I just experienced something similar. I installed Thunderbird In my Samsung tablet running android 14. I added 3 mailboxes, no unified inbox. I did a force close, still no unified inbox. I had to force close a total of 5 times before the unified inbox showed up.
This is a bug.
 
Expected behavior: When setting up a second account, the inbox should be enabled automatically. However, adding a third account should preserve existing settings, specifically respecting users who have disabled unified inbox configuration.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/preferences/UnifiedInboxConfigurator.kt`
